reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21: Demucs music source separation proven FIRST in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web WASM fp32) - the model loads (STFT ops supported), separates a stereo segment into 4 stems in ~9.5 s, the stems SUM back to the mixture (reconstruction corr 0.90), and content separates (bass beat -> bass stem 0.95). Then the deployed page driven end-to-end. Sample media is first-party (synth beat) + public-domain (JFK speech); users upload their own songs.

rubric.evidence: Feasibility + correctness verified in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21 WASM, fp32) BEFORE building. htdemucs.onnx loads (STFT/DFT ops supported), takes [1,2,343980] stereo -> [1,4,2,343980] four stems in ~9.5 s/segment. On a controlled mix (spoken voice over a synth beat) the 4 stems SUM back to the mixture (reconstruction corr 0.90 - proves the model ran), the bass beat isolates to the BASS stem (corr 0.95), and the voice separates into a stem. Deployed page (scripts/validate-music-source-separation.mjs 8/8) renders the 4 real stems with playback + download. No canned audio.
rubric.notes: DISTINCT from the built speech-separation (ConvTasNet separates two SPEAKERS): this splits MUSIC into instrument/vocal stems.

rubric.evidence: Input raw 44.1 kHz STEREO waveform [1,2,343980] -> output 4 waveform stems [1,4,2,343980] = [drums, bass, other, vocals]. A distinct audio-to-audio I/O: the STFT lives inside the ONNX graph, so we feed waveform and get waveform stems back (no manual spectrogram maths, unlike a Spleeter-style pipeline).
rubric.notes: The browser decodes/resamples any file to 44.1 kHz stereo and trims to the model's fixed 7.8 s window.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (MrCitron/demucs-v4-onnx / htdemucs, linked), task (audio source separation), the 4 stems, input (44.1 kHz stereo, first ~7.8 s), architecture (Hybrid Transformer Demucs), quantisation (fp32, run via onnxruntime-web), backend (WASM), download (~303 MB) and license (cc-by-nc-4.0). The How-it-works block shows the real ORT call + tensor shapes. No invented API.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only, like the other raw-ORT demos.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'MrCitron/demucs-v4-onnx', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:303}). The 303 MB ONNX is fetched THROUGH Cache Storage under a key carrying the model-id path -> lib/model-cache.js auto-initialises on a returning visit with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~303 MB) with byte progress. Verified: fresh profile showed Download; clicking it flipped to ready.
rubric.notes: Large model, so the never-silently-download guarantee matters; the shared loader + auto-init on return both hold via fetchCached.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (~303 MB, byte progress) -> ready (controls enabled) -> separating (~10 s status) -> 4 stems with transport. Separation failures surface on the page in a role=status .err region, not only the console. Verified download -> ready -> separate -> play/mute end-to-end.
rubric.notes: The 'Separating — about ten seconds' status sets expectations honestly for the WASM decode.

rubric.evidence: Upload (drag-drop + Choose-audio) or a built-in sample; then Play/Stop, three presets (Full mix / Karaoke / Acapella), a per-stem include checkbox (the live mix), and a per-stem WAV download. Every control is labelled and keyboard-operable; the drop zone gives drag feedback; no dead controls.
rubric.notes: The checkboxes ARE the mixer - unchecking vocals gives karaoke, checking only vocals gives an acapella; presets just set the checkboxes.

rubric.evidence: Every stem is the model's real separated waveform (playable + downloadable); the readout shows the live backend + separation time + segment length. The page is HONEST about limits: it separates the first ~7.8 s segment per run, and Demucs's vocals stem is trained on SUNG vocals so a spoken voice lands in 'other' - the page says so and tells users to upload a song with singing to isolate real vocals.
rubric.notes: The sample is a first-party synth beat + public-domain speech - no licensed music is bundled or faked.

rubric.evidence: The status is role=status aria-live; every button and the per-stem checkboxes have accessible names (e.g. 'Include Vocals in playback'); stems are labelled with text (emoji is decorative aria-hidden); download links are real <a download>. Colour/emoji are never the only signal - the stem name is always text.
rubric.notes: Audio is inherently the output medium; the transport + per-stem controls are all keyboard-operable and named.

rubric.evidence: The drop zone, transport, presets and stem rows all wrap; each stem row is a grid that stays legible on narrow screens; the per-stem waveform canvas is width:100%. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate 8/8), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: The stem-row grid (checkbox / name / waveform / download) reflows without overflow on mobile.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; separation of a 7.8 s segment is ~9.5 s on WASM single-thread (measured) - close to real-time and acceptable with the 'about ten seconds' status. The ~303 MB download is one-time (cached).
rubric.notes: It's an 80M-param hybrid transformer; a WebGPU path (follow-up) and multi-segment streaming for full songs would help, but one fast segment keeps the demo snappy.

guidanceConsulted.query: drag and drop audio upload with an accessible file picker and a built-in sample
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offer accessible input paths (a real file input + drag-drop), give drag feedback, and provide a zero-setup sample.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — a labelled <input type=file> + Choose-audio button + a drop zone with drag feedback + a one-click built-in sample (first-party synth beat + public-domain speech).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: upload + drop + sample all work; drop rejects non-audio

guidanceConsulted.query: run heavy audio inference off the main thread and keep the UI responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Do heavy work off the main thread; set expectations for long tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — separation runs in a module Worker; a 'Separating — about ten seconds' status sets expectations; playback uses Web Audio buffer sources.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: UI stays responsive during the ~10 s separation

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible transport + per-stem controls, colour not the only signal
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce state in aria-live, name every control, and never encode meaning in colour/emoji alone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live; named checkboxes/buttons; stem names are text (emoji aria-hidden decorative).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: controls are named + keyboard-operable; stems readable as text

openQuestions: Would a WebGPU backend bring separation well under real-time so a full song could be streamed segment by segment?
Is there a cleanly-licensed (permissive or NC) SUNG-vocals sample clip that could ship to demonstrate vocal isolation without relying on user upload?
followUpGoals: Add a WebGPU path and multi-segment streaming so a whole track can be separated, not just the first 7.8 s.
Offer a one-click 'download karaoke mix' (sum of the enabled stems) in addition to per-stem WAVs.
Source a short cleanly-licensed sung-vocals clip to showcase true vocal isolation in the built-in sample.
summary: Music source separation (Demucs / htdemucs) — a distinct audio family: split a song into drums/bass/other/vocals stems on-device, vs the built ConvTasNet speech-separation (which splits two SPEAKERS). Proven in the browser on onnxruntime-web WASM fp32 (loads, separates a 7.8 s segment in ~9.5 s, stems reconstruct the mix at corr 0.90, bass beat isolates to the bass stem). ~303 MB, cc-by-nc-4.0 (acceptable — the catalogue already builds NC/NC-SA families; only ND/absent-license are blocked). Upload/drop/sample; mix-and-match stems + karaoke/acapella presets + per-stem WAV download; index demo complete + verified 8/8. Honest that it does the first ~7.8 s per run and that the vocals stem needs SUNG vocals. A WebGPU path + full-song streaming + a sung-vocals sample are recorded follow-ups.
